title: Rubus longior
taxonomy: Rubus longior is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Rubus, which includes blackberries and raspberries. The plant is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. As a member of the Rubus genus, it is closely related to other species of brambles and thorny shrubs. Within the Rubus genus, Rubus longior is specifically classified as a type of blackberry.

benefits: Rubus longior, a type of blackberry bush, is believed to have several health benefits. It is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body against free radicals and oxidative stress. properties: Rubus longior, also known as the Japanese black raspberry, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its therapeutic properties. It contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, which could aid in the prevention and treatment of various infections.
